phrases.intent_en: Ask about luxury shopping on the avenue
phrases.text_local: Où sont les boutiques de luxe sur les Champs ?
phrases.pronunciation: oo sohn lay boo-TEEK duh LOOKS soor lay SHAHNZ
phrases.cultural_note: Louis Vuitton's flagship is at number 101. Cartier, Guerlain, and Tiffany's are also here. Most luxury shops are between Rond-Point and the Arc de Triomphe.

responses.english: The luxury shops are at the top of the avenue, toward the Arc.
responses.phonetic: lay boo-TEEK duh looks sohn ahn oh duh lah-vuh-NOO, vehr lark
responses.french: Les boutiques de luxe sont en haut de l'avenue, vers l'Arc.

phrases.intent_en: Warn about pickpockets on the avenue
phrases.text_local: Faites attention à vos affaires ici, il y a des pickpockets.
phrases.pronunciation: FET ah-tahn-SYOHN ah vohz ah-FEHR ee-SEE, eel ee ah day peek-poh-KET
phrases.cultural_note: The Champs-Élysées is the top pickpocket zone in Paris. Keep bags closed and in front. Be wary of clipboard petitions and people offering 'found' rings — all are scams.

responses.english: Thanks for the advice. I will be careful.
responses.phonetic: mehr-SEE doo kohn-SAY. zhuh feh ah-tahn-SYOHN
responses.french: Merci du conseil. Je fais attention.

phrases.intent_en: Ask for a reasonably priced café
phrases.text_local: Il y a un café avec des prix raisonnables dans le coin ?
phrases.pronunciation: eel ee ah uhn kah-FAY ah-VEK day PREE reh-zoh-NAHBL dahn luh KWAHN
phrases.cultural_note: Cafés on the avenue itself are notoriously overpriced (8-10 euros for an espresso). Walk one block to a side street for normal Parisian prices. Or try the Publicis Drugstore for a middle ground.

responses.english: Go to the side streets, it is cheaper than the terraces here.
responses.phonetic: ah-LAY dahn lay roo oh-TOOR, seh mwahn shehr kahn teh-RASS ee-SEE
responses.french: Allez dans les rues autour, c'est moins cher qu'en terrasse ici.

phrases.intent_en: Ask about the Grand Palais nearby
phrases.text_local: Le Grand Palais est loin d'ici ?
phrases.pronunciation: luh grahn pah-LEH eh lwahn dee-SEE
phrases.cultural_note: The Grand Palais is just off the Champs at Avenue Winston Churchill. Check for major exhibitions — it hosts world-class art shows, fashion events, and the annual ice rink.

responses.english: The Grand Palais is at the bottom of the avenue, on the right.
responses.phonetic: luh grahn pah-LEH eh tahn bah duh lah-vuh-NOO, soor lah drwaht
responses.french: Le Grand Palais est en bas de l'avenue, sur la droite.

phrases.intent_en: Ask where the public toilets are on the avenue
phrases.text_local: Il y a des toilettes publiques sur l'avenue ?
phrases.pronunciation: eel ee ah day twah-LET poo-BLEEK soor lah-vuh-NOO
phrases.cultural_note: Public Sanisettes are available along the avenue. Cafés typically require a purchase to use their toilets.

responses.english: Yes, there are public toilets at the bottom of the avenue.
responses.phonetic: wee, eel ee ah day sah-nee-ZET ahn bah duh lah-vuh-NOO
responses.french: Oui, il y a des Sanisettes en bas de l'avenue.
